new_terms.context: A metric used in energy modeling to estimate the true contribution of a power plant to grid reliability.
The process and waiting list for new power generation or load facilities to connect to the electrical grid.
Private entities that own and operate power plants to sell electricity to the grid, such as Vistra or Constellation.
Financial contracts for the future delivery of electricity, used to hedge or speculate on prices.
The increase in electrical demand over time, specifically from AI datacenters in this context.
Specific phases of the LLM inference process mentioned in the 'Power to Prefill, Dirt to Decode' tagline.
